name: Glass Transition in Polymers
description: On cooling an amorphous polymer, free volume contracts until, below the glass transition temperature, segmental motion freezes and the material becomes a rigid glass; cooling rate and plasticizer content shift the observed Tg.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Amorphous polymer melt"]
    N2["Cool the polymer"]
    N3["Free volume decreases"]
    N4{"Below Tg?"}
    N5["Segmental motion frozen"]
    N6["Glassy, brittle solid"]
    N7["Rubbery / mobile state"]
    N8["Plasticizer / rate shifts Tg"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|yes| N5
    N5 --> N6
    N4 -->|no| N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|modifies| N3

sources.title: Nature of the glass transition and the glassy state
